records.ety: [AS. fetor, feter; akin to OS. feterōs, pl., OD. veter, OHG. fezzera, Icel. fjöturr, L. pedica, Gr. pe`dh, and to E. foot. √ 77. See Foot.]

defs: A chain or shackle for the feet; a chain by which an animal is confined by the foot, either made fast or disabled from free and rapid motion; a bond; a shackle.
quotes: [They] bound him with fetters of brass.

defs: Anything that confines or restrains; a restraint.
quotes: Passion's too fierce to be in fetters bound.

records.ety: [imp. & p. p. Fettered (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Fettering.]

defs: To put fetters upon; to shackle or confine the feet of with a chain; to bind.
quotes: My heels are fettered, but my fist is free.

defs: To restrain from motion; to impose restraints on; to confine; to enchain; as, fettered by obligations.
quotes: My conscience! thou art fettered More than my shanks and wrists.
